cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A nerve cell that receives and transmits electrical and chemical signals

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Branching extensions of a neuron that receive incoming signals from other neurons

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The long fiber extending from the cell body that transmits electrical impulses away from the soma

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The fatty insulating layer around axons that speeds up neural transmission via saltatory conduction

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The tiny gap between the axon terminal of one neuron and the dendrite of the next where neurotransmitters cross

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Chemical messengers released into the synapse that bind to receptor sites on the postsynaptic neuron

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: An electrical impulse that travels down the axon when the neuron's membrane reaches threshold

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The principle that a neuron either fires at full strength or does not fire at all

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The minimum level of stimulation required to trigger a neural impulse

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A brief period after firing during which a neuron cannot generate another action potential

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The process by which neurotransmitters are reabsorbed by the presynaptic neuron, ending the signal

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Drugs that mimic or enhance the effects of a neurotransmitter

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Drugs that block or reduce the effects of a neurotransmitter

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Dopamine
cards.answer: Neurotransmitter involved in reward, motivation, pleasure, and motor control

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Serotonin
cards.answer: Neurotransmitter that regulates mood, sleep, and appetite; low levels linked to depression

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Acetylcholine (ACh)
cards.answer: Neurotransmitter involved in muscle activation, attention, and memory formation

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: GABA
cards.answer: The brain's main inhibitory neurotransmitter; reduces neural activity and calms anxiety

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Glutamate
cards.answer: The brain's main excitatory neurotransmitter; involved in learning and memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Endorphins
cards.answer: Natural opioid neurotransmitters that reduce pain and produce feelings of pleasure

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Norepinephrine
cards.answer: Neurotransmitter involved in alertness, arousal, and the fight-or-flight response

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The brain and spinal cord together

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The sensory and motor neurons connecting the central nervous system to the rest of the body

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The division of the PNS that controls voluntary skeletal muscle movements

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The division of the PNS that controls involuntary functions like heart rate, digestion, and breathing

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The branch of the autonomic nervous system that arouses the body for fight-or-flight responses

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The branch of the autonomic nervous system that calms the body after stress and promotes rest-and-digest

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A simple, automatic response to a sensory stimulus processed at the spinal cord level

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The body's slow chemical communication system using hormones secreted by glands into the bloodstream

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The endocrine gland in the brain that regulates other glands and is controlled by the hypothalamus

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Endocrine glands atop the kidneys that release epinephrine and norepinephrine during stress

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The wrinkled outer surface of the brain responsible for higher-order thinking, language, and voluntary movement

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Frontal lobe
cards.answer: Brain lobe behind the forehead; controls planning, decision-making, personality, and voluntary movement

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Parietal lobe
cards.answer: Brain lobe at the top-back of the head; processes touch, temperature, pressure, and spatial awareness

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Temporal lobe
cards.answer: Brain lobe above the ears; processes auditory information and is involved in memory and language comprehension

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Occipital lobe
cards.answer: Brain lobe at the back of the head; processes visual information from the eyes

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Region in the left frontal lobe that controls speech production; damage results in halting, effortful speech

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Region in the left temporal lobe that controls language comprehension; damage results in fluent but meaningless speech

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Amygdala
cards.answer: Limbic system structure that processes emotions, especially fear and aggression

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Hippocampus
cards.answer: Limbic system structure essential for forming new explicit memories; damage causes inability to form new memories

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Hypothalamus
cards.answer: Brain structure that regulates body temperature, hunger, thirst, sleep, and controls the pituitary gland

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Thalamus
cards.answer: The brain's sensory relay station that routes incoming sensory information to the appropriate cortical area (except smell)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Cerebellum
cards.answer: Brain structure at the back of the skull that coordinates voluntary movement, balance, and motor learning

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The brainstem structure that controls heartbeat and breathing

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The brainstem structure that controls arousal and attention, acting as a net-like filter for incoming stimuli

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The thick band of nerve fibers connecting the left and right cerebral hemispheres

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The brain's ability to reorganize neural pathways in response to learning, experience, or injury

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Brain imaging technique that measures blood flow changes to detect active brain areas during tasks

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Brain imaging technique that uses radioactive tracers to show metabolic activity in the brain

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Recording of electrical activity in the brain using electrodes placed on the scalp

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Brain imaging technique that uses magnetic fields to produce detailed images of brain structures

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Tissue destruction technique used to study the function of specific brain areas by damaging them

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Roger Sperry
cards.answer: Split-brain research revealing lateralization of brain functions

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Michael Gazzaniga
cards.answer: Continued split-brain research with Sperry; demonstrated hemisphere specialization

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Paul Broca
cards.answer: Discovered Broca's area — the brain region controlling speech production

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Carl Wernicke
cards.answer: Discovered Wernicke's area — the brain region controlling language comprehension

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Phineas Gage
cards.answer: Railroad worker whose frontal lobe injury revealed the role of the frontal lobe in personality and decision-making

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The specialization of each cerebral hemisphere for particular cognitive functions

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Non-neuronal cells in the nervous system that support, nourish, insulate, and protect neurons

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Neurons that carry information from sensory receptors to the central nervous system

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Neurons that carry signals from the central nervous system to muscles and glands

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Neurons within the brain and spinal cord that connect sensory and motor neurons

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Neurotransmitter deficit linked to Alzheimer's disease

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Neurotransmitter excess linked to schizophrenia

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Neurotransmitter deficit linked to Parkinson's disease

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The area of the frontal lobe that controls voluntary body movements

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The area of the parietal lobe that processes body touch and position sensations

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The set of brain structures (amygdala, hippocampus, hypothalamus) involved in emotion, motivation, and memory

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The study of how genes interact with the environment to influence behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The proportion of variation among individuals in a group attributable to genetic factors

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Research method comparing identical and fraternal twins to estimate genetic influence on traits
